name: All-American Pie Pastry
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup Frozen lard or shortening (cut into bits)
recipeInstructions:
To ensure a tender crust, this recipe should not be doubled. Thoroughly freeze the lard or shortening for the flakiest results.
1. Add the flour and salt to the bowl of a food processor and pulse 2-3 times.
2. Scatter the frozen lard or shortening over the flour mixture.
3. Sprinkle ice water evenly over the dough.
4. Pulse the food processor until the dough just begins to form into a ball.
5. Shape the dough into a ball, then flatten slightly.
6. Dust with flour and place in the center of a 2-gallon-size plastic storage bag.
7. Refrigerate for up to 40 minutes.
8. With the bag open and the dough centered, roll out the dough into a circle that almost touches the edges of the bag.
9. Discard the top sheet of the plastic bag; lay the rolled dough onto the pie plate.
10. Peel away the plastic wrap.
11. Trim the pastry to hang about an inch over the rim.
12. Press the overhanging pastry under (between the pastry and the pie tin).
13. Fold the excess pastry back into the ball, then pinch or crimp the edges in a zigzag or fluted pattern.
Yield: 1 single-crust 8- to 10-inch pie shell.
